a: An error that indicates the alternative text for an image is too long.
An error that indicates that no image object is included, but the request indicates that the header should be placed above the image.
An error that indicates the endpoint doesn’t support app transaction identifiers instead of transaction identifiers.
An error that indicates the text for a bullet point is too long.
An error that indicates the image size provided is invalid.
An error that indicates the URL is invalid.
An error that indicates there are too many bullet points.
An error that indicates the transaction ID is invalid.
An error that indicates the transaction ID provided is not an original transaction ID.
An error that indicates that the endpoint doesn’t support transactions for products the customer receives through Family Sharing.
An error that indicates the body text is too long.
The text and its bullet-point image to include in a retention message’s bulleted list.
Configures a default message for a specific product in a specific locale.
Configures the URL for your Get Retention Message endpoint in the sandbox and production environments.
Refer to these data types for request and response payloads.
The decoded request body the App Store sends to your server to request a real-time retention message.
The request body that contains the default configuration information.
The response body that contains the default configuration information.
An error that indicates a default message isn’t configured.
Deletes a default message for a product in a locale.
Deletes a previously uploaded image.
Deletes a previously uploaded message.
Deletes the URL for your Get Retention Message endpoint, in the sandbox or production environments.
Understand the error codes that Retention Messaging API responses return.
An error that indicates an error with an existing test.
A map of server-to-server notification failure reasons and counts that represent the number of failures during a performance test.
An error that indicates that passing a performance test is required before you can set a URL for the production environment.
An error that indicates a general internal error.
Gets the default message for a specific product in a specific locale, if it’s configured.
Gets the image identifier and state for all uploaded images.
Gets the message identifier and state of all uploaded messages.
Gets the results of the performance test for the specified identifier.
Gets the URL for real-time messages that points to your Get Retention Message endpoint, which you previously configured.
A response that contains status information for all images.
An image identifier and state information for an image.
A response that contains status information for all messages.
A message identifier and status information for a message.
An error that indicates the header text is too long.
Recognize the rate limits that apply to Retention Messaging API endpoints, and handle them in your code.
An error that indicates the image identifier already exists.
An error that indicates the image is currently in use as part of a message, so you can’t delete it.
An error that indicates the image isn’t in the approved state, so you can’t configure it as part of a default message.
An error that indicates the system can’t find the image identifier.
Initiates a performance test of your Get Retention Message endpoint in the sandbox environment.
An error that indicates the image that’s uploading is invalid.
An error that indicates the locale is invalid.
An error the API returns that indicates the performance test request is invalid.
An error that indicates the product ID is invalid.
An error that indicates the request ID is invalid.
A decoded JSON Web Signature (JWS) header.
An error that indicates when you reach the maximum number of uploaded images.
An error that indicates when you reach the maximum number of uploaded messages.
An error that indicates the message identifier already exists.
An error that indicates the message isn’t in the approved state, so you can’t configure it as a default message.
An error that indicates the system can’t find the message identifier.
An object that enumerates the test configuration parameters.
The request object you provide for a performance test that contains an original transaction identifier.
The performance test response object.
An object that describes test response times.
An object the API returns that describes the performance test results.
An error the API returns if the service can’t find the specified test run.
The status of the performance test.
An error that indicates the request exceeded the rate limit.
The request body the App Store server sends to your Get Retention Message endpoint.
A response you provide to choose, in real time, a retention message the system displays to the customer.
An error that indicates that the URL for your endpoint isn’t configured.
The request body for configuring the URL of your Get Retention Message endpoint.
The response body that contains the URL for your Get Retention Message endpoint.

t: Responding to real-time retention messaging requests
a: Select retention messages for customers in real time by responding to requests on your Get Retention Message endpoint.
Provide a reason for customers to stay subscribed with a preconfigured message that you can choose in real time, appropriate to the product and locale.
Learn about new features and updates in the Retention Messaging API.
Upload images and messages for retention messaging, configure default messages, and complete the setup for promotional-offer and switch-plan messages.

t: Setting up your Get Retention Message endpoint
a: Choose retention messages for customers in real time by implementing an endpoint on your server that responds to requests from the App Store server.
Uploads an image to use for retention messaging.
Uploads a message to use for retention messaging.
The definition of an image with its alternative text.
The request body for uploading a message, which includes the message text and an optional image reference and bullet points.
A Base64-encoded JSON object which contains a JWS with information describing an offer or switch-plan recommendation.
A response object you provide to present an offer or switch-plan recommendation message.
An algorithm you use to sign a JSON Web Signature (JWS).
The alternative text for a corresponding image.
A switch-plan message and product ID you provide in a real-time response to your Get Retention Message endpoint.
The unique identifier of an app in the App Store.
The body text you provide for a message.
The text you provide for an individual bullet-list item.
The server environment, either sandbox or production.
The header text you provide that appears above the body text in a message.
The position where the header text appears in a message.
A unique identifier for an image that you provide when you upload the image.
The size of an image.
The approval state of an image.
A string that represents a locale short code.
A message identifier you provide in a real-time response to your Get Retention Message endpoint.
A unique identifier for a message, which you provide when you upload the message.
The approval state of the message.
The original transaction identifier of an In-App Purchase.
A unique identifier for a product, which you create in App Store Connect.
A promotional offer and message you provide in a real-time response to your Get Retention Message endpoint.
The promotional-offer signature you generate using an earlier signature version.
The promotional-offer signature you generate in a JSON Web Signature (JWS) format.
A string that contains the URL you provide for your Get Retention Message endpoint.
The identifier of the performance test request.
A unique identifier the App Store server creates for its requests.
The success or error information the App Store server records when it attempts to send an App Store server notification to your server.
The UNIX time, in milliseconds, that the App Store signed the JSON Web Signature data.
The payload in a JSON Web Signature (JWS) format, signed by the App Store.
A JSON Web Signature (JWS) header parameter that contains the certificate chain that corresponds to the key you use to digitally sign the JWS.
